management_notes:
Westhaven Gold's management team, led by CEO Gavin Dirom, delivered two important milestones in 2025-2026: a fully updated PEA for the Shovelnose Gold Project (March 2025) and the signing and closing of a definitive earn-in agreement with Dundee Corporation (signed December 2025, closed February 2026). The Dundee deal — whereby Dundee can earn up to a 60% interest in Shovelnose through staged expenditures totalling CDN$85 million, with a firm CDN$30 million committed — is a meaningful endorsement of the project's potential from a sophisticated Canadian mining financier.

A precise insider ownership breakdown was not confirmed through SEDAR+ filings reviewed for this research cycle. The management team has been consistent in exploring the Spences Bridge Gold Belt since the company's inception and has grown the Shovelnose resource from initial discovery to a multi-zone PEA-level project through disciplined, systematic drilling. The CEO's ability to negotiate a meaningful earn-in with Dundee — which carries forward exploration costs without diluting Westhaven shareholders via equity — is a positive capital allocation signal.

Score: 3/5. Good track record of project advancement and value-accretive deal making, offset by the absence of confirmed insider ownership data and the early-development nature of the company.
geology_notes:
Westhaven's Shovelnose Gold Project hosts a combined mineral resource (effective date February 28, 2025, supporting the updated 2025 PEA, NI 43-101 technical report filed April 17, 2025) of 677,200 oz Au Indicated and 270,300 oz Au Inferred across three zones: South, Franz, and FMN. The average mine-grade is 5.26 g/t Au with a silver credit of 32 g/t Ag. The deposit style is low-sulphidation epithermal, which is the same mineralisation type as several of BC's historic high-grade gold mines.

The Spences Bridge Gold Belt (SBGB) is a greenfields discovery belt in southern BC, with Westhaven controlling approximately 70% of the prospective land package. The deposit is amenable to underground mining based on the PEA mine design (637,000 oz payable Au over 11.1 years). An active 2025 drill program added results including 70.79 metres at 1.13 g/t Au (SN25-442, South Zone) with higher-grade intervals, demonstrating continued resource expansion potential. The 2026 program includes infill and geotechnical drilling ahead of a planned Pre-Feasibility Study.

Score: 3/5. The Indicated resource (677,200 oz at 5.26 g/t) is solid for a BC epithermal project, and the PEA demonstrates economic viability. The study tier is PEA only — no reserve conversion, ±35-50% accuracy. The Inferred component (270,300 oz) represents ~29% of total resources. A PFS is the next required step before reserve classification becomes possible.
capital_notes:
The Dundee Corporation earn-in agreement is the defining capital factor for WHN. Under the terms signed December 2025 and closed February 2026, Dundee has the exclusive right to earn up to 60% of the Shovelnose project by funding CDN$85 million in staged project expenditures, with a firm CDN$30 million committed to the first phase. This structure means Westhaven does not need to access equity capital markets to fund Shovelnose development for the foreseeable future — Dundee carries the exploration and development costs.

The 2026 planned expenditures of CDN$20 million — including infill drilling, geotechnical work, environmental baselines, and preliminary engineering studies for a PFS — are Dundee's financial responsibility under the first-phase commitment. Westhaven shareholders retain 100% of the project value until Dundee earns in, then retain at least 40% after full earn-in. The structure is non-dilutive to WHN share count (earn-in is through project expenditures, not equity issuance in WHN).

The downside is that once Dundee earns its full 60%, Westhaven becomes a 40% passive partner in a project whose full value requires CDN$85M+ of additional spend before construction decision. Shares outstanding grew approximately 30.3% in the past year (to fund corporate overhead and exploration ahead of the Dundee deal), indicating historical dilution. The Dundee deal substantially improves the forward capital outlook. Score: 4/5.
catalyst_notes:
The Dundee earn-in closing in February 2026 was the most significant recent catalyst — it validated Shovelnose's potential to a sophisticated counterparty and funded the next phase of exploration and engineering without shareholder dilution. The 2026 program ($20 million funded by Dundee) includes infill drilling, geotechnical work, and engineering studies to support a PFS — with the PFS itself as the next major value-creation milestone.

Drill results from the 2026 season (infill and step-out at South Zone, FMN, and Franz zones) will provide ongoing news flow. The final 2025 drilling results (January 2026) included 8.05 metres at 6.18 g/t Au within a broader 70.79m intercept — consistent with project quality and a positive technical signal. The resource update to be completed as part of the PFS process could add ounces from zones still in active exploration.

The PFS commencement announcement (expected 2026) would be a major catalyst, upgrading the project from PEA to PFS level and materially improving NAV quality. However, a completed PFS is likely a 2027 event. There are no near-term production or feasibility study completion events. Score: 3/5 — a solid forward calendar, but the next step-change catalyst (completed PFS) is likely 12-18 months away.
acquisition_notes:
Westhaven's acquisition value analysis requires two adjustments: (1) the 50% PEA discount to the stated NPV, and (2) the impact of the Dundee earn-in diluting Westhaven's ownership to 40% upon full earn-in completion. The 2025 PEA reports an after-tax NPV6% of CDN$454 million at USD$2,400/oz gold (base case). Applying a 50% PEA discount: adjusted project NAV = CDN$227 million. Westhaven's 40% retained interest after full Dundee earn-in: CDN$227M × 40% = CDN$90.8 million.

At current market price of approximately CA$0.25/share, with an estimated ~200 million shares outstanding (based on 30.3% annual dilution from a prior-year base), implied market capitalisation is approximately CDN$50 million. This gives an adjusted P/NAV of approximately CDN$50M / CDN$90.8M = 0.55x on a 40% retained interest, 50% PEA-discounted basis. Before the Dundee earn-in is fully completed, Westhaven still holds 100% and the unadjusted discounted NAV is CDN$227M — implying P/NAV of 0.22x on the project's full current value.

At current gold prices of USD$4,637/oz vs the PEA base of USD$2,400/oz, the real project NPV is substantially higher, making this discount even more pronounced. The analyst consensus target of CA$1.10 (vs CA$0.25 current price — 340% upside implied) suggests the market is significantly undervaluing the asset. Score: 4/5. The extreme discount to PEA-derived NAV (even on a conservative basis) and the Dundee deal validation justify a 4/5 score despite the PEA study tier. Note: shares outstanding figure is estimated (~200M); confirmed share count not available in this research cycle.
analyst_summary:
Westhaven Gold earns a WATCH with a composite score of 17/25. The core thesis is a high-grade epithermal gold discovery in southern British Columbia — 677,200 oz Au Indicated at 5.26 g/t, with a 2025 PEA showing CDN$454 million after-tax NPV (43.2% IRR) — now funded by a CDN$85 million Dundee Corporation earn-in agreement that eliminates near-term equity dilution risk. At CA$0.25/share, the market capitalisation is approximately CDN$50 million, which represents a dramatic discount to any reasonable estimate of project value — whether at 100% ownership (P/NAV ~0.22x on 50% PEA-discounted basis) or at Westhaven's eventual 40% retained interest (P/NAV ~0.55x).

The key risks are study tier (PEA only — PFS is the next required milestone, expected to complete in 2027) and the dilution from the Dundee earn-in, which caps Westhaven's long-term ownership at 40%. The stock grew its share count by ~30% in the past year to fund pre-Dundee costs. Confirmed insider ownership was not established in this research cycle. The analyst consensus target of CA$1.10 implies the market's current pricing is not reflective of the project's fundamental value.

The key catalyst to watch is the PFS commencement and resource update announcement (likely H2 2026) incorporating 2026 Dundee-funded infill drilling. A positive PFS at current gold prices would demonstrate a project NPV well above the current enterprise value and would likely trigger significant re-rating. The Spences Bridge Gold Belt remains an emerging district with significant discovery potential in the broader WHN land package.
